headline: Core bridge contracts bytecode-verified (Exact Match) with public OP Stack source repo; recognized-firm audit coverage identified but most-recent confirmed audit (Spearbit 2025-03) predates deployed implementations (Oct–Dec 2025), leaving audit drift unresolved
short_headline: Verified; audit drift vs deployed versions

findings.text: L1StandardBridge proxy (0x3154Cf16ccdb4C6d922629664174b904d80F2C35) is verified on Etherscan with 'Source Code (Proxy)' label, pointing to implementation 0x61525EaaCDdB97D9184aFc205827E6A4fd0Bf62A, which is verified 'Contract Source Code Verified (Exact Match)' as 'L1StandardBridge' v2.8.0, Solidity 0.8.15.

OptimismPortal proxy (0x49048044D57e1C92A77f79988d21Fa8fAF74E97e) shows 'Similar Match Source Code' for a standard EIP-1967 Proxy contract — per V1 rules this is acceptable for standard proxy patterns and does not constitute a verification gap. Implementation 0x97cEbbf8959e2A5476fbe9B98A21806Ec234609B is verified 'Source Code Verified (Exact Match)' as 'OptimismPortal2' v5.2.0, Solidity 0.8.15.

Both assessed proxy contracts (L1StandardBridge and OptimismPortal) have independently verified implementation contracts with Exact Match verification; no unverified-implementation risk exists for these two contracts.

The deployed source code for L1StandardBridge v2.8.0 imports from 'src/L1/ProxyAdminOwnedBase.sol', 'src/universal/ReinitializableBase.sol', and 'src/universal/StandardBridge.sol', all consistent with the ethereum-optimism/optimism public repository. No specific commit SHA was pinned in this run — no direct fetch of the repository was performed due to robots.txt restrictions.

The ethereum-optimism/optimism security-reviews directory (referenced in search results) documents audits covering L1StandardBridge and OptimismPortal2: Trust (Dec 2023, op-contracts/v1.2.0), Cantina (Feb 2024, op-contracts/v1.3.0), Sherlock Fault Proofs contest (Mar 2024, OptimismPortal2), and Spearbit Upgrade 13 (Feb/Mar 2025, op-contracts/v2.0.0). No protocol.audit_links were provided in the per-protocol context.
Auditing firms include Spearbit, Cantina, Trail of Bits, and Sherlock — all recognized in Solidity security. Trust and Coinbase Protocol Security also appear in the OP Stack review record.

The deployed L1StandardBridge implementation (v2.8.0) was created on 2025-10-23 and OptimismPortal2 (v5.2.0) on 2025-12-22. The most recently identified audit covering these contract types is Spearbit Upgrade 13 (op-contracts/v2.0.0, ~Feb/Mar 2025), which predates both deployments by at least 7–10 months. No audit specifically confirming coverage of v2.8.0 or v5.2.0 was recoverable in this run. Additional audits covering later op-contracts releases may exist but are not confirmed.

steelman.red: No audit links were provided in the per-protocol context, and the Etherscan page for the OptimismPortal2 implementation explicitly states 'No Contract Security Audit Submitted'; the most recently confirmed audit covering these contracts (Spearbit, ~Mar 2025) predates the deployed implementations by 7–10 months with no confirmed re-audit for v2.8.0/v5.2.0.
steelman.orange: All key contracts are bytecode-verified Exact Match on Etherscan with source clearly corresponding to the open-source OP Stack repo, and recognized firms (Spearbit, Cantina, Sherlock) have conducted multiple audits on the OP Stack bridge contracts; however, the deployed versions (L1StandardBridge v2.8.0 deployed Oct 2025, OptimismPortal2 v5.2.0 deployed Dec 2025) appear to post-date the most recently identified audit (op-contracts/v2.0.0, Mar 2025) by more than 6 months, leaving post-audit drift unresolved.
steelman.green: The Base Bridge is the canonical OP Stack built on ethereum-optimism/optimism — a continuously and publicly audited codebase by top-tier firms; all contracts are Exact Match verified with a fully public source repo, and it is plausible that more recent audits covering the deployed versions exist but were not reachable in this run due to GitHub robots.txt restrictions.

rationale.verdict: Choosing orange because verified bytecode (Exact Match for both key implementations) and a clearly public, well-audited source repo (OP Stack) support high confidence in the code itself, but the most recently identified audit for the relevant contract types (Spearbit, ~Mar 2025 for op-contracts/v2.0.0) predates the deployed L1StandardBridge v2.8.0 (Oct 2025) and OptimismPortal2 v5.2.0 (Dec 2025) by more than 6 months, satisfying the orange criterion of 'audit scope stale relative to deployment'; no re-audit of these specific versions was confirmable in this run.

evidence.shows: Official Base documentation confirming bridge.base.org is deprecated and the canonical bridge (OP Stack L1StandardBridge / OptimismPortal) is the primary bridge infrastructure for Ethereum ↔ Base

Official deployed contract addresses for Ethereum Mainnet L1 side: L1StandardBridge 0x3154Cf16ccdb4C6d922629664174b904d80F2C35, OptimismPortal 0x49048044D57e1C92A77f79988d21Fa8fAF74E97e, L1CrossDomainMessenger 0x866E82a600A1414e583f7F13623F1aC5d58b0Afa, ProxyAdmin 0x0475cBCAebd9CE8AfA5025828d5b98DFb67E059E, and admin addresses (Proxy Admin Owner: 0x7bB41C3008B3f03FE483B28b8DB90e19Cf07595c Gnosis Safe; Guardian: 0x09f7150D8c019BeF34450d6920f6B3608ceFdAf2 Gnosis Safe; SystemConfig owner: 0x14536667Cd30e52C0b458BaACcB9faDA7046E056 Gnosis Safe)
L1StandardBridge proxy labeled 'Base: Base Bridge' with 'Source Code (Proxy)' verification; implementation pointed to 0x61525EaaCDdB97D9184aFc205827E6A4fd0Bf62A

L1StandardBridge implementation verified 'Contract Source Code Verified (Exact Match)', contract name L1StandardBridge, version 2.8.0, Solidity 0.8.15+commit.e14f2714, optimization 999999 runs; full source code readable and imports from src/L1/ and src/universal/ paths consistent with ethereum-optimism/optimism repo; deployed 2025-10-23 at block 23642890

OptimismPortal proxy labeled 'Base: Base Portal'; shows 'Similar Match Source Code' for standard EIP-1967 Proxy contract (acceptable per V1 for recognized proxy pattern); implementation at 0x97cEbbf8959e2A5476fbe9B98A21806Ec234609B

OptimismPortal2 implementation verified 'Source Code Verified (Exact Match)', contract name OptimismPortal2, version 5.2.0, Solidity 0.8.15; deployed 2025-12-22 at block 24069615; Etherscan lists 'No Contract Security Audit Submitted' for this specific address (refers to Etherscan's audit submission feature, not the OP Stack audit program)

Base Security Council structure: 12 entities (Coinbase + 11 independents), 9-of-12 multisig quorum for contract upgrades, Stage 1 decentralization reached April 2025; Security Council Gnosis Safe identified as Proxy Admin Owner (0x7bB41C3008B3f03FE483B28b8DB90e19Cf07595c) per the contracts page

OP Stack security reviews index listing multiple audits covering L1StandardBridge and OptimismPortal/OptimismPortal2: Trust (Dec 2023, op-contracts/v1.2.0), Cantina (Feb 2024, op-contracts/v1.3.0), Sherlock fault-proofs contest (Mar 2024), Spearbit Upgrade 13 (Feb 2025, op-contracts/v2.0.0); page was reachable via search-result snippet but blocked by robots.txt for direct fetch

unknowns: V2: Source-to-repo commit SHA not pinned — GitHub robots.txt prevented direct repo fetch; bytecode diff not performed; correspondence is inferred from visible imports matching ethereum-optimism/optimism repo structure

V3-offchain: No audit links were provided in the per-protocol context; the full current security-reviews index was not directly fetchable; additional audits covering op-contracts versions after v2.0.0 (which would cover the deployed L1StandardBridge v2.8.0 and OptimismPortal2 v5.2.0) may exist but were not confirmable in this run

V5: Post-audit drift status for the specific deployed versions (L1StandardBridge v2.8.0 deployed 2025-10-23; OptimismPortal2 v5.2.0 deployed 2025-12-22) relative to the most recently identified audit (Spearbit, ~Mar 2025 for op-contracts/v2.0.0) could not be resolved; no audit for these exact versions was found in confirmable sources

admin_addresses.role: Proxy Admin Owner (L1) — Security Council multisig
Batch Sender (EOA managed by Coinbase Technologies)

protocol_metadata.about: Base Bridge is the canonical Ethereum-to-Base bridge built on the OP Stack. Users deposit ETH or ERC-20 tokens on Ethereum by locking them in the L1StandardBridge (with ETH held in the OptimismPortal) and receiving equivalent assets on Base, or initiate withdrawals from Base that are proven and finalized on Ethereum via a 7-day fault-proof challenge window. The system uses fault-proof dispute games (OptimismPortal2) for trustless L2-to-L1 message verification, with an emergency Guardian multisig and a 9-of-12 Security Council multisig controlling contract upgrades.

headline: Core bridge contracts are explorer-verified, but no audit evidence was pinned or found on explorer audit sections

findings.text: The assessed canonical Ethereum-Base bridge entrypoints were discovered from Base's official contract-address page: L1StandardBridge at 0x3154Cf16ccdb4C6d922629664174b904d80F2C35 on Ethereum, L2StandardBridge at 0x4200000000000000000000000000000000000010 on Base, and OptimismPortal at 0x49048044D57e1C92A77f79988d21Fa8fAF74E97e on Ethereum. Their explorer pages report verified source for the proxy contracts and disclose current implementation addresses.

The current implementations checked for the assessed proxies are separately verified: L1StandardBridge implementation 0x61525EaaCDdB97D9184aFc205827E6A4fd0Bf62A is an Etherscan exact match, L2StandardBridge implementation 0xC0d3c0d3c0D3c0d3C0D3c0D3C0d3C0D3C0D30010 is a BaseScan genesis bytecode match, and OptimismPortal implementation 0x97cEbbf8959e2A5476fbe9B98A21806Ec234609B is an Etherscan exact match.

The public ethereum-optimism/optimism repository contains corresponding L1StandardBridge and L2StandardBridge source files with matching contract names and standard-bridge semantics, but this run did not pin a deployment commit or perform bytecode equivalence.

No audit report URL was pinned in protocol.audit_links for this run, and the explorer pages checked for the assessed proxy/implementation contracts show 'No Contract Security Audit Submitted' in their contract audit sections.
The assessed bridge contracts are upgradeable proxies, but the current implementation contracts identified by the explorers were checked separately and are verified.

steelman.red: Red is justified because the slice requires recognized audit coverage for green/orange-quality verifiability, protocol.audit_links was empty, and explorer audit sections for checked contracts show no submitted contract audit.
steelman.orange: Orange could be argued because the main proxies and implementations are verified and public OP Stack source exists, while the missing audit linkage and unpinned repo correspondence are material gaps rather than opaque bytecode.
steelman.green: Green would require verified proxy and implementation bytecode, a corresponding public source repo, and a recognized audit covering the currently deployed contracts with acceptable drift, but the audit requirement is not satisfied by the evidence.

rationale.verdict: Choosing red because source verification is good, but the audit side of the verifiability rubric fails: protocol.audit_links was empty for this run, no audit scope/date/commit could be inspected, and every checked explorer contract page reported no submitted contract security audit.

evidence.shows: Base's bridge documentation describes Ethereum-to-Base bridging options, notes bridge.base.org has been deprecated, and points users toward Superchain bridge frontends and programmatic bridging.

Official Base contract-address page lists Base Mainnet L2 bridge contracts, Ethereum Mainnet L1 contracts including L1StandardBridge, OptimismPortal, L1CrossDomainMessenger, ProxyAdmin, and Base admin addresses including Gnosis Safe roles.

Etherscan labels this address as Base Bridge / L1StandardBridge proxy; the contract page reports Source Code Verified Exact Match, contract name L1ChugSplashProxy, implementation 0x61525EaaCDdB97D9184aFc205827E6A4fd0Bf62A, and no contract security audit submitted.
Etherscan reports Source Code Verified Exact Match for the implementation contract named L1StandardBridge and shows no contract security audit submitted.

BaseScan labels this address as Base L2 Standard Bridge Proxy, reports source-code status for the proxy, shows implementation 0xC0d3c0d3c0D3c0d3C0D3c0D3C0d3C0D3C0D30010, and shows no contract security audit submitted.
BaseScan reports Source Code Verified Genesis Bytecode Match for the implementation contract named L2StandardBridge and shows no contract security audit submitted.

Etherscan labels this address as Base Portal / OptimismPortal proxy, reports Source Code Verified Similar Match for the proxy, shows implementation 0x97cEbbf8959e2A5476fbe9B98A21806Ec234609B, and shows no contract security audit submitted.
Etherscan reports Source Code Verified Exact Match for the implementation contract named OptimismPortal2 and shows no contract security audit submitted.
Public OP Stack source repository contains L1StandardBridge.sol; the file declares contract L1StandardBridge and describes it as responsible for transferring ETH and ERC20 tokens between L1 and L2.

Public OP Stack source repository contains L2StandardBridge.sol; the file declares contract L2StandardBridge, includes the Base predeploy address 0x4200000000000000000000000000000000000010, and describes it as responsible for transferring ETH and ERC20 tokens between L1 and L2.

Base vulnerability-reporting page says potential vulnerability reports can be submitted via HackerOne and that Coinbase's bug bounty program covers the Base network, Base bridge contracts, and Base infrastructure; it also mentions Optimism's Immunefi Bedrock bounty for the OP Stack framework.
HackerOne bug bounty page was reachable as the linked Coinbase program page, although the fetched body required JavaScript.

Base Security Council documentation says Base launched decentralized control of contract upgrades via a Security Council, requires 9 of 12 entities to approve upgrades, and lists current signer entities/addresses.

unknowns: V1: DeFiPunkd ABI API probe for discovered addresses could not be opened in this run because web.run rejected generated API URLs as unsafe; block-explorer pages were used instead.
V2: No specific deployment commit SHA was pinned, and no local compile or bytecode diff was performed to prove exact source-to-repo correspondence for the deployed bridge implementations.
V3: protocol.audit_links was empty in the provided run context, so no audit URL could be opened to verify auditor, audit date, in-scope contracts, or commit.
V4: Auditor recognition could not be assessed because no audit report was linked or otherwise verified in the allowed evidence set.
V5: Post-audit drift could not be assessed because no most-recent in-scope audit commit/version was available.

protocol_metadata.about: Base Bridge is the canonical OP Stack bridge path for moving ETH and ERC20 assets between Ethereum L1 and Base L2. Users deposit through L1 bridge/portal contracts and withdraw through the L2 bridge plus cross-domain messaging; L1-native ERC20s are escrowed on L1 and represented on L2, while the bridge contracts coordinate mint/burn or release flows across domains. Base's old bridge.base.org UI is deprecated, but the underlying standard bridge contracts remain the programmatic settlement layer.

headline: Bytecode verified on Etherscan (proxy + impl) with public OP Stack source; no pinned/fetched recognized-firm audit covering deployment
short_headline: Verified proxy/impl, public repo, audit unconfirmed

findings.text: Proxy contract at 0x3154Cf16ccdb4C6d922629664174b904d80F2C35 (L1ChugSplashProxy) has Source Code Verified on Etherscan (compiler v0.8.15+commit.e14f2714, optimization enabled 999999 runs); implementation at 0x61525EaaCDdB97D9184aFc205827E6A4fd0Bf62A (L1StandardBridge) is separately Source Code Verified with full ABI and matching OP Stack bridge functions (depositETH, bridgeERC20, finalizeBridgeETH, paused, etc.).

Explorer-visible source structure and ABI exactly match L1StandardBridge and related contracts in the public ethereum-optimism/optimism repository (OP Stack canonical bridge implementation used by Base); no exact commit SHA pinned and no bytecode diff performed.
Verified proxy (L1ChugSplashProxy) with independently verified implementation contract (L1StandardBridge).

steelman.red: No audit reports are listed in the provided protocol.audit_links (none recorded) and none were fetched in this run that cover the currently deployed contracts and version; without explicit recent audit evidence from a recognized firm, full outsider verifiability of deployed behavior cannot be confirmed at green level.
steelman.orange: Strong positive signals from block-explorer verification of both proxy and implementation plus direct structural/ABI correspondence to the recognized public OP Stack repository, but the absence of any fetched or pinned recognized-firm audit report scoped to the live deployment (with drift assessment) blocks a green grade per the explicit criteria.
steelman.green: Bytecode is fully verified on Etherscan for proxy and implementation, source is publicly available and matches in the canonical OP Stack repo used across audited L2s including Base, and the bridge is part of the extensively reviewed OP Stack ecosystem with known historical audits by top firms.

rationale.verdict: Choosing orange because explorer verification (V1/V6) and public-repo correspondence (V2) provide robust, independently checkable evidence of what the deployed code does, yet the green requirement of ≥1 recognized-firm audit covering the currently-deployed contracts (≤6 months drift or re-audited) is not satisfied by any evidence URL or pinned link in this run; red would undervalue the concrete on-chain and source transparency that does exist.

evidence.shows: Official Base documentation explicitly lists the L1StandardBridge (canonical bridge) contract address on Ethereum Mainnet as 0x3154Cf16ccdb4C6d922629664174b904d80F2C35.

Contract labeled 'Base: Base Bridge' / L1ChugSplashProxy; Source Code Verified; compiler v0.8.15+commit.e14f2714; optimization enabled with 999999 runs; constructor argument _owner = 0x6606D3c20CC94cc7aA1D430C0E83a5129976153A; it is a proxy with implementation address 0x61525EaaCDdB97D9184aFc205827E6A4fd0Bf62A.

Implementation contract is L1StandardBridge; Source Code Verified; full ABI and creation code provided, containing all standard OP Stack bridge view/write functions (MESSENGER, depositETH, bridgeERC20To, finalizeBridgeETH, paused, version, initialize, etc.) exactly matching the deployed proxy's expected interface.

Canonical public repository containing the complete source for OP Stack contracts (including L1StandardBridge.sol, StandardBridge, CrossDomainMessenger integration, and proxy patterns) used as the implementation basis for Base's canonical bridge; structure and function signatures correspond directly to the Etherscan-verified source.

unknowns: V2: exact commit SHA (or bytecode equivalence diff) matching the deployed contracts to a specific commit in ethereum-optimism/optimism not identified or performed in this run

V3: no audit_links provided in per-protocol context; no recognized-firm audit report (Trail of Bits, OpenZeppelin, Spearbit, etc.) URL fetched or verified for scope/dates covering the current L1StandardBridge deployment

V5: post-audit drift (new functions, access-control changes, or accounting modifications since last in-scope audit) cannot be assessed without a pinned audit commit/version and current deployment commit

protocol_metadata.about: Canonical bridge enabling users to deposit ETH and ERC-20 tokens from Ethereum L1 to Base L2 (and withdraw back) via the OP Stack L1StandardBridge proxy contract; transfers are secured directly by Ethereum consensus with a standard 7-day withdrawal challenge window and no additional validators or oracles.
